title: Clearing for Lightsheet Microscopy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"20so9","text":"This protocol was developed for whole mount nanobody staining and organic clearing of mouse brains for fluorescence lightsheet microscopy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8tmol","text":"Prepare the needed buffers and solutions in advance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ffq1t","text":"Handle toxic reagents (e.g. paraformaldehyde, sodium hydroxide, diclormethane) with care! Read the safety data sheets before use and follow the recommendations for safe handling and storage.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"olbbz","text":"Please read the safety data sheets before use and handle reagents with care.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In case of intravenous vessel labeling:
Anesthetize mouse and inject vessel dye i.v. or a dye combination in accordance with legal requirements for animal experiments.
Euthanize mouse in accordance with legal requirements for animal experiments until it shows no pedal reflexes anymore, open the thorax, expose the heart.
Inject...

25 mL heparinized 1xPBS ( 10 U/mL Heparin sodium salt from porcine intestinal mucosa Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #H3149 (CAS: 9041-08-1) ) at Room temperature liver becomes pale, yellowish and/or light brown
150 mL 4% PFA solution (cf. step 2), ice-cold (fresh or freshly thawed aliquot only)

Dissect the organ of interest immediately, postfix it in 4% PFA solution for 2 h (for bones: 4 h) at 4 °C check individual fixation times here: Klingberg A, Hasenberg A, Ludwig-Portugall I, Medyukhina A, Männ L, Brenzel A, Engel DR, Figge MT, Kurts C, Gunzer M (2017). Fully Automated Evaluation of Total Glomerular Number and Capillary Tuft Size in Nephritic Kidneys Using Lightsheet Microscopy.. https://doi.org/10.1681/ASN.2016020232
Wash 3x with 1xPBS at 40 rpm, Room temperature, 30 minutes, orbital shaker
Store in 1xPBS + 0.05 % NaN 3 at 4 °C for up to 6 months OR start immediately.
clears hemoglobin, if necessary, should not be combined with tomato lectin i.v. staining
Incubate the dissected organ in 30 % volume decolorization solution (cf. step 3) for 48:00:00 at Room temperature while gentle shaking
for bony tissues only; adjust time if needed
Incubate the whole skull (cleared from jaws and muscles) in PBS/EDTA ( 22.5 mL 0.5M EDTA Invitrogen Catalog #AM9260G + 27.5 mL 1xPBS) at Room temperature for 48:00:00 , gentle shaking
Incubate in permeabilization buffer containing normal goat serum at 37 °C Overnight , mild shaking/rotating improves the result
Nanobody staining (repeat the following steps depending on sample size): I repeated this once for a total of 2 staining steps for whole mouse brain.

4.5 mL permeabilization buffer 500 µL Goat serum Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G9023 5 µL FluoTag®-X4 anti-GFP (AbberiorStar 635P) NanoTag Biotechnologies Catalog #N0304 at 37 °C for 120:00:00 , mild shaking/rotating
Or
Wash 1 3x wash for 02:00:00 at Room temperature with 1xPBS + 0.1 % volume Triton® X 100 Carl Roth Catalog # 3051 (CAS: 9002-93-1) and mild shaking/rotating repeat Overnight
Wash 2 3x wash for 02:00:00 at Room temperature with 1xPBS and mild shaking/rotating repeat Overnight
Dehydration
Recommended for bone 50% EtOH/dH2O for 12:00:00 70% EtOH for 12:00:00 100%EtOH for 12:00:00 100% EtOH for 12:00:00
Recommended for brain
In 50% EtOH/dH2O Overnight , rotation at Room temperature
In 70% EtOH/dH2O Overnight , rotation at Room temperature
In 100% EtOH Overnight , rotation at Room temperature
In 100% EtOH Overnight , rotation at Room temperature
Please note : at least butanol, isopropanol, methanol or tetrahydrofurane can also be used or even combined (one by one) but the effects on the tissue (e.g. shrinkage) can differ.
Delipidation (optional, recommended for lipid-rich organs as brains) incubate for 04:00:00 in dichloromethane Dichlormethane Contributed by users

Clearing Incubate in Dibutylether Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #5.89583 or Ethyl cinnamate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #112372 for at least 24:00:00 replace the solvent at least once after 24 h to improve clearing result

Samples can be stored quite long in the organic solvent (keep water-free!) without losing quality @ RT in the dark. Samples are imaged in the same solution or solutions with equal refractive index and characteristics.

In general: Use only hand-made PFA solutions (without stabilizing reagents). Dehydrating reagents can be changed/combined. Organic solvents can be changed/combined. Dehydrating and clearing are reversible. Organize your panels as following: Use fluorophores emitting at longer wavelength (red or far-red). Small structures: as “red” as possible, bigger structures can eventually be stained with “green” fluorophores. Avoid lipid-based/organic dyes (=fluorescein and cyanine-based dyes; better to use AF dyes, DyLight or new synthetic dyes (Atto/Abberior Star)). Never ever bring the cleared organs in contact with water! Keep fluorescently labeled samples in the dark. organic solvents are not compatible with PE or PS plastic ware! Solely use PP or glass ware! Always test solvent stability first. Always handle reagents with care. This protocol is a so-called „organic-based clearing“ and parts may be exchangeable with comparable protocols like iDisco, uDisco, FluoClearBABB, PEGASOS, THF/DBE,… Miltenyi offers kits and special antibodies for this procedure as well.

Prick the BD Vacutainer Blood Collection Set into the left ventricle (light red) and snip with small scissors in the right atrium (the bigger the snip the better it runs and clears). BD Vacutainer® Safety-Lok™ Blood Collection Set (Blood Collection Set, BD, 367282, 21G, https://www.aponeo.de/01111606-bd-vacutainer-safety-lok-21-g-18-cm-blutentn-set.html?src=ggl.pla&em_src=kw&em_cmp=google&gad_source=1&gclid=EAIaIQobChMI5bXMgc76jAMV05FoCR0CNiPCEAQYDiABEgIwQvD_BwE , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/tktrcmgzp.jpg )
Phosphate buffered saline (1xPBS)
4% Paraformaldehyde solution (4% PFA solution)

Prepare 10x PBS first: 2 g Kalium chloride, KCl Carl Roth Catalog # 6781.1 (CAS: 7447-40-7) 2 g Kalium dihydrogen phosphate ChemSolute Catalog #16480250 14.42 g Di-sodium hydrogen phosphate dihydrate ChemSolute Catalog #8622 80 g Sodium Chloride ChemSolute Catalog #13671000 fill up to 1 L with any distilled water and stirr until dissolved.
For 1xPBS , 10xPBS is diluted 1:10 with any distilled water. pH 7.2 - pH 7.4

40 g paraformaldehyde AppliChem Catalog #A3813.1000 100 mL 10xPBS (prepared in step 1.1) Heat up (<70°C!) while stirring under the hood for ~ 04:00:00 - 05:00:00 ; let it cool down; add ~ 250 µL 10M sodium hydroxide solution Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #72068 (adjust to pH 8 to preserve endogenous fluorescent proteins). Store in aliquots at -20 °C and thaw slowly just before use (overnight in fridge and directly use).
Decolorization solution (if needed)

12.5 g N,N,N,N-Tetrakis(2-hydroxypropyl)ethylenediamine TCI Chemicals Catalog #T0781 (CAS: 102-60-3) 12.5 g Urea powder Carl Roth Catalog #7638 (CAS: 57-13-6) 7.5 g Triton® X 100 Carl Roth Catalog # 3051 (CAS: 9002-93-1) --> fill up to 50 mL with 1xPBS; dissolve in water bath if needed --> dilute in 1xPBS for use (final concentration 30 % volume )
Wash afterwards 3x 00:30:00 at Room temperature
Wash afterwards 3x 00:30:00 at Room temperature
Permeabilization buffer

add 1.5 % volume Goat serum Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G9023 when permeabilization buffer is prepared in aliquots for several days in advance: add goat serum freshly just before use!

0.5 % volume Triton® X 100 Carl Roth Catalog # 3051 (CAS: 9002-93-1) 0.5 millimolar (mM) Methyl-β-Cyclodextrin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #332615 (CAS: 128446-36-6) 0.2 % volume N-Acetyl-L-hydroxyprolin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #441562 (CAS: 33996-33-7) 0.05 % volume NaN 3 --> dissolve in 1xPBS

4.5 mL permeabilization buffer 500 µL Goat serum Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G9023 5 µL FluoTag®-X4 anti-GFP (AbberiorStar 635P) NanoTag Biotechnologies Catalog #N0304 1 µL TO-PRO™-1 Iodide (515/531) - 1 mM Solution in DMSO Thermo Fisher Catalog #T3602 at 37 °C for 120:00:00 , mild shaking/rotating

Recommendations for nanobodies: http://discotechnologies.org/vDISCO/ Please note: Antibodies labeled with fluorophores emitting at longer wavelength decrease background signals. No fluorescein/cyanine-based fluorophores (organic dyes) – there are publications using FITC (?) Antibody penetration time and efficiency strongly depend on tissue composition. Antibody concentration strongly depends on availability/expression of antigen. Small antibodies like nanobodies (Chromotek/NanoTag) strongly improve the staining outcome.
creator: Hanna Edler
